data.title: What problem does Hermes Agent solve?
data.description:
Author: Zhang Feng

In 2026, the global AI Agent track officially bid farewell to the conceptual bubble and entered the deep waters of the industry, focusing on framework competition and practical application.. ...span>..

I. The phenomenal rise of the open-source track triggers a wave of Agent migration across the entire domain

The widespread popularity of Hermes Agent is not a coincidence or a result of hype, but rather a result of technological breakthroughs combined with the resonance of industry pain points(iv) Ecosystem Value Closed Loop

Underlying framework open source → developers co-create skills applications → users use it in diverse scenarios → user feedback feeds back into kernel iteration → more vendors connect to improve infrastructure → attract more developers to join, forming a positive cycle ecosystem, breaking away from dependence on a single project, and achieving self-growth of the ecosystem.

(iv) Ecosystem Value Closed Loop

VI. A Differentiated Showdown Between Two Industry Giants, with Clearly Differentiated Paths The current global open-source agent market has formed a dominant duopoly: Hermes Agent and OpenClaw. While benchmarking against closed intelligent agent products such as Claude Code and OpenAI Codex, each player exhibits significant differences in their underlying approaches, capability focuses, and applicable scenarios. A comprehensive horizontal comparison clearly reveals their respective positioning and value boundaries. (I) Core Competitors: Hermes Agent VS OpenClaw These two represent two completely different technological paths in the industry. They are not competing in a comprehensive manner, but rather complement each other in terms of capabilities. A detailed comparison is as follows: Hermes is a self-evolving intelligent engine, focusing on Agent self-growth, deep execution, and experience accumulation; OpenClaw is... The multi-channel gateway scheduling platform focuses on multi-terminal access, task distribution, and tool link management. In industry parlance: OpenClaw manages the entry channels, Hermes manages the intelligent brain. Memory System: Hermes uses a four-layer local persistent database for cross-month information retrieval and long-term user modeling; OpenClaw relies solely on file-based short-term memory, lacks native long-term storage, suffers from severe cross-session forgetting, and has no positive correlation between usage duration and capabilities. Skill Mechanism: Hermes features self-generated, automatically iterating private skills, accumulating abilities from tasks; OpenClaw relies on manually uploaded pre-set plugins, with all skills coming from the community marketplace, and carries significant security risks from malicious plugins. Model and Deployment: Hermes offers seamless full-model compatibility, strong private local deployment, and high data security; OpenClaw boasts a rich ecosystem of plugins and numerous access channels, but many rely on cloud operation, posing a high risk of data leakage and making it prone to crashing in complex tasks. Applicable Scenarios: Hermes is suitable for long-term personal digital partners, enterprise private processes, permanent automated operations and maintenance, and complex tasks that require continuous growth; OpenClaw is suitable for one-time lightweight tasks, multi-platform message scheduling, rapid prototyping development, and lightweight programming tool calls. (II) Comparison with Other Competitors Claude Code: A closed-system, dedicated intelligent agent with high execution efficiency but deeply bound to the Anthropic model, lacking model selection rights, long-term memory, and autonomous evolution capabilities. It only serves its own ecosystem and has extremely poor versatility. OpenAI Codex: A dedicated intelligent agent focused on the programming field, with strong system-level control capabilities, but limited to vertical scenarios, lacking general productivity capabilities, closed-source and fee-based, and with high commercialization barriers. Domestic native closed-source agents: Most rely on their own large models for closed development, resulting in closed ecosystems, poor compatibility, high customization costs, lack of open-source foundations, and difficulty in secondary expansion. In summary, Hermes leads in all dimensions: long-term memory, autonomous evolution, data security, model compatibility, and private deployment. OpenClaw has advantages in plugin ecosystem, number of channels, ease of use, and lightweight execution speed. Closed commercial intelligent agents are limited to their own ecosystems, and their versatility is far inferior to the two open-source giants. VII. The technology is not yet mature, and there are still many shortcomings in industrial implementation. Although Hermes Agent overcomes many fundamental pain points in the industry and has outstanding overall strength, as a new-generation framework that has only been online for two months, it still has obvious technical shortcomings, ecosystem defects, and difficulties in industrial implementation, significantly limiting its industry development. First, the project version is relatively new, and the overall technical maturity is insufficient. Currently, it's only updated to version v0.8, and the kernel is still in a rapid iteration phase. The stability of some complex, long-chain tasks is insufficient, planning logic is prone to deviations in extreme scenarios, and the collaborative capabilities of complex multi-agent clusters are not yet perfect. There is still room for optimization before reaching a large-scale enterprise-level highly reliable production environment. Secondly, the native plugin ecosystem lags far behind OpenClaw. OpenClaw has a very mature plugin market after long-term development, with abundant off-the-shelf tools and resources. The Hermes ecosystem is mainly based on community-developed skills, with fewer general-purpose off-the-shelf plugins, insufficient scenario coverage, and a lack of resources for beginners to use out of the box. Initial use requires a certain amount of secondary development costs. Third, the inference overhead is relatively high, resulting in a relatively slow execution speed. Influenced by the multi-layered memory system, the autonomous review and evolution module, and the security sandbox verification mechanism, the computational power consumption per task is higher, and the execution speed of simple, short tasks is slower than that of lightweight gateway frameworks, making it less efficient in lightweight scenarios. Fourth, there is the issue of skill library redundancy and retrieval burden. With prolonged use, the number of locally accumulated skills continues to increase, easily leading to issues such as skill redundancy, invocation conflicts, and decreased retrieval efficiency. The framework currently lacks a robust mechanism for intelligent skill simplification and automatic cleanup of expired skills, resulting in rising long-term operation and maintenance costs. Fifth, common industry-wide challenges have not been fully resolved. The framework still cannot completely eliminate industry-wide pain points such as the illusion of large-scale underlying models, the inexplicability of complex, long-chain decision-making processes, and limited adaptability to highly complex cross-industry businesses. Simultaneously, the overall global industrial-scale deployment rate of AI intelligent agents remains low, making it difficult for enterprises to realize ROI, and the widespread commercialization of the ecosystem is still constrained by the overall industry environment. Sixth, the risk of confusion caused by projects with the same name and the problem of abuse of application boundaries. There are crypto trading bots and on-chain protocol projects with the same name across the entire network, which can easily cause confusion for users; at the same time, some users abuse the framework's API capabilities to conduct virtual currency transactions, crossing regulatory red lines and posing application compliance risks.

VIII. From Personal Digital Partner to Distributed General-Purpose Intelligent Infrastructure

Combining technological iteration, ecosystem expansion, and industry trends, and leveraging its underlying architectural advantages, the Hermes Agent's future development path is clear. It will evolve along four major directions:Technology Improvement, Ecosystem Expansion, Scenario Deepening, and Industry Popularizationcontinuously expanding the value boundaries of general-purpose intelligent agents.

First, the core technology will continue to iterate, addressing any shortcomings in maturity.

Subsequent versions will focus on optimizing the stability of complex task planning, reducing inference computing power overhead, improving the intelligent skill management mechanism, strengthening multi-Agent cluster collaboration capabilities, and improving the fault tolerance and rollback mechanism for long-term task execution, gradually reaching enterprise-level high-reliability production standards and bridging the industry gap from "usable" to "reliable." Simultaneously, the security system will be deepened, improving full-process auditing, access control, and risk interception, adapting to the compliance requirements of highly sensitive industries such as finance and government. Secondly, the ecosystem will continue to expand, achieving dual improvement in community and localization. On the one hand, the official and community skill libraries will be enriched, supplementing out-of-the-box application resources and narrowing the gap with leading framework ecosystems; on the other hand, the adaptation to Chinese scenarios will be continuously deepened, deeply integrating with the domestic office ecosystem and enterprise digital systems, improving the full-link native compatibility of domestic large models, and completing comprehensive localization. Simultaneously, it deepens the cross-framework collaborative ecosystem, forming a complementary and collaborative industry system with frameworks such as OpenClaw, jointly promoting the overall development of the open-source agent industry. Third, application scenarios are expanding from personal productivity to enterprise-wide implementation. Initially, it focuses on personal digital assistants, developer tools, and lightweight operation and maintenance automation; in the mid-term, it comprehensively penetrates SME process automation, internal office digital employees, and business data processing; in the long term, it enters the deployment of private intelligent agents in large enterprises, undertaking internal system scheduling, autonomous execution of business processes, and cross-system data collaboration, becoming the underlying intelligent infrastructure for enterprise digital transformation. Fourth, the business model is deepened, constructing a complete open-source business closed loop. Building upon existing subscription, licensing, and customization services, we will improve the skills market revenue sharing system, expand new business models combining distributed computing power networks with the framework, and leverage our own model technology to create an integrated full-stack service of "large model + intelligent agent + computing power," forming a sustainable and high-growth business system. This will validate the commercialization feasibility of general-purpose open-source intelligent agents and provide a commercialization model for the entire industry. Fifth, we will move towards a distributed general-purpose intelligent infrastructure. In the long term, as multi-agent collaboration technology matures, Hermes will evolve from a single intelligent agent framework into a distributed personal and enterprise intelligent network kernel. With self-evolution and long-term memory capabilities at its core, it will connect various software, hardware, and business systems, becoming the general-purpose intelligent foundation for the next-generation digital world, undertaking the large-scale general collaboration needs in the early stages of AGI implementation. The competition in the AI ​​intelligent agent field in 2026 is no longer a superficial contest of the number of tools and plugins, but a profound revolution in the field encompassing underlying architecture, memory capabilities, autonomous intelligence, data security, and the business ecosystem. Looking at the industry as a whole, the vast majority of general-purpose intelligent agents remain trapped in the fundamental dilemma of "being able to converse, but difficult to execute, lacking memory, unable to grow, and difficult to implement," merely completing a simple encapsulation of large-scale model capabilities without touching the true core value of intelligent agents. The rise of Hermes Agent is essentially a return to the value of architecture. It doesn't blindly pile up tools and interfaces, but precisely tackles the five core pain points of the industry: memory loss, inability to evolve, data insecurity, model binding, and unclear commercialization. With its self-evolving kernel, layered persistent memory, full model compatibility, private security, and a healthy open-source business ecosystem, it redefines the product standard for general-purpose intelligent agents. It doesn't just solve the tool needs of a single scenario, but rather the systemic problems of the entire industry's general-purpose intelligent agents: "execution without accumulation, dialogue without intelligence, framework without ecosystem, and product without commercial viability." Of course, the road to technological maturity is long and arduous, and the project still faces many unresolved challenges related to ecosystem, speed, and stability. However, from an industry development perspective, Hermes Agent has forged a new path for the development of general-purpose intelligent agents, distinct from the gateway scheduling route. With "growing together with users" as its core, it drives AI to transform from a one-off dialogue tool into a long-term, autonomously evolving, and deeply collaborative digital partner. This provides a new and feasible model for the large-scale deployment and widespread civilian application of AI intelligent agents globally, and lays a solid open-source foundation for the construction of next-generation general artificial intelligence infrastructure.

data.title: Bankless: rsETH Attack Shakes the DeFi Industry
data.description:
Author: Jack Inabinet, Senior Analyst at Bankless; Translator: @JinseFinancexz

As the cryptocurrency industry struggles to cope with the latest DeFi security vulnerability, the entire sector is forced to confront a life-or-death question: Are DeFi applications truly secure?

Last weekend, major cryptocurrency lending markets suffered the largest DeFi vulnerability attack of the year.

A skilled attacker illegally minted 116,500 rsETH by breaching Kelp DAO's LayerZero-based cross-chain bridge.

These newly minted tokens (without any actual asset backing), worth approximately $290 million, were subsequently deposited into mainstream lending protocols such as Aave and used as collateral to borrow hundreds of millions of dollars worth of ETH, ultimately resulting in bad debts and triggering an industry-wide liquidity crisis.

I. Root Cause of the Incident

This attack unfolded rapidly in two phases: The attackers first successfully exploited a vulnerability in Kelp DAO's LayerZero-based cross-chain bridge, and then siphoned hundreds of millions of dollars from Aave using unbacked rsETH.

Phase 1: LayerZero Defenses Collapse

Kelp DAO's rsETH cross-chain bridge relies on LayerZero's messaging infrastructure.

The key issue is that Kelp DAO used the lowest-security configuration mode during integration—a 1/1 decentralized validator network (DVN) setup. This gave a single validator node operated by LayerZero Labs full authority to approve cross-chain messages.

Although LayerZero claimed in its post-mortem analysis that it had warned against using the minimum security configuration and recommended that high-value cross-chain bridges adopt a multi-validator scheme, its protocol still allows a 1/1 deployment mode. More notably, approximately 47% of protocols on LayerZero use the same configuration. The attacker exploited this single point of failure to forge a legitimate cross-chain message, inducing the cross-chain bridge operated by LayerZero to directly mint 116,500 rsETH tokens without any actual asset backing to an address controlled by the attacker. Although Kelp DAO's multisignature contract immediately froze the core contract, it was too late to reverse the subsequent damage... [Image of the image: https://img.jinse.com.cn/7454300_watermarknone.png](https://img.jinse.com.cn/7454300_watermarknone.png) [Image of the image: jq5A67ukc7VJT1g9WSZ0rXzVWpMfSVcxTht76OcK.png](https://img.jinse.com.cn/7454300_watermarknone.png)](https://img.jinse.com.cn/7454300_watermarknone.png) [Image of the image: jq5A67ukc7VJT1g9WSZ0rXzVWpMfSVcxTht76OcK.png)](https://img.jinse.com.cn/7454300_watermarknone.png) [Image of the image: h3 style="text-align: left;">Phase Two: Aave Liquidity Withdrawal

With the stolen tokens, the attackers immediately deposited a large amount of their rsETH into Aave V3 (while also depositing smaller amounts into other platforms such as SparkLend and Fluid).

Through this fictitious collateralized position, the attackers were able to borrow a massive amount of WETH using tokens with no real value as collateral, thereby causing an estimated $262 million in bad debt for Aave lenders in related transactions. Faced with this risk, astute DeFi lenders did not sit idly by and let bad debt erode their positions; instead, driven by panic, they collectively withdrew funds over the weekend. Following the attack, major protocols lost over $7 billion in assets, with Aave alone experiencing a $6.2 billion outflow, representing approximately 23% of the total value locked (TVL) in the lending market. The market panic was so severe that the collateralization ratios in multiple ETH, USDC, and USDT markets on Aave V3 surged to 100%, effectively freezing liquidity and preventing users from withdrawing further assets.

II. Current Situation

With billions of dollars in assets effectively frozen in the cryptocurrency lending market, risks are compounding and escalating.

Depositors are unable to actively manage their positions because their collateral has already been lent out, while interest rates, affected by capital utilization, are soaring, further exacerbating borrowers' position pressure. As liquidity dries up and panic spreads, market concerns about further accumulation of bad debts and the further spread of DeFi risks are growing daily.

To control the losses, the Aave governance mechanism has completely disabled the rsETH market in versions V3 and V4. However, this is a remedial measure; before recovering from this serious vulnerability, the protocol still needs to deal with its hundreds of millions of dollars in outstanding bad debts. III. What will happen next? Aave V3's staking module currently holds $201 million in stablecoins and $56 million in WETH, which can be used for write-offs to help absorb the deficit caused by rsETH. In addition, the protocol's original security module contains $266 million worth of AAVE tokens, which can be sold to cover the remaining shortfall. While the size of these reserves suggests that Aave should be able to absorb the loss without becoming insolvent, the rsETH vulnerability has raised deeper concerns about the resilience of the decentralized lending market. A shock of this scale could discourage users from lending funds—let alone absorbing risk through staking/security modules—potentially undermining confidence in the unified liquidity strategy upon which Aave V3 is built. The crypto-economic system should be built on a resilient, trust-minimized foundation. However, in the competition for a smoother user experience or a more dazzling set of features, some teams have taken shortcuts, introducing vulnerable single points of failure. The collapse of the LayerZero cross-chain bridge in Kelp DAO, which uses 1-of-1 validator nodes, is a prime example. Such incidents highlight the risks of poorly designed, quasi-centralized systems and reveal the potentially enormous costs of taking shortcuts. If the crypto world is to deliver on its promises, builders must abandon fragile architectures and return to the principle of security first, rather than relying on fragile multi-signature or single-signer architectures.

data.title: Code is dead, logic is immortal: How to determine "structural plagiarism" in the AI ​​era
data.description:
Author: Merkle3s Capital; Source: X,@Merkle3sCapital

Introduction

Code is dead, logic is immortal.

This is not alarmist. By 2026, AI will be able to easily translate JavaScript into Python, rewrite Go into Rust, and eliminate all traces of code plagiarism. But the isomorphism of architecture cannot be eliminated by rewriting—just like translating a book from Chinese to English, the story structure remains the same.

... In April 2026, a war erupted in the AI ​​open-source community over who invented the self-evolving agent first. On one side was Nous Research, a top global open-source AI lab with $70 million in funding and 85,000 stars on GitHub; on the other was a small Chinese team called EvoMap. This was the first real-world test of intellectual property judgment standards in the AI ​​era: When code comparison fails, what else can we rely on to determine plagiarism? Chapter 1: The Complete Timeline of the Event From February 1st to April 14th, 74 days, the entire process of a small Chinese team's technological innovation and the development by a top global lab. Timeline Comparison: From the release of core concepts (February 1-16) to the launch of the Hermes Skills Ecosystem (March 12), a period of 24-39 days has elapsed. Nous Research claims its main repository was created in July 2025. However, verification shows that the allegedly "self-evolving" module was publicly created on March 9, 2026—36 days later than Evolver.

Chapter Two: What is the Hermes Agent?

How a top AI lab with $70M funding turned an "uncensored model" into a "7/24 self-evolving agent."

Nous Research: Who is building this agent?

Funding exceeds $70M (including a $50M Series A), 85,000 Stars on GitHub. Their most famous product is the Hermes LLM series—a large language model known for its "uncensored" nature; VentureBeat praised it as "outperforming ChatGPT without content restrictions." Now, what they're doing is transforming Hermes from a "model" into an "Agent." Core Positioning: 24/7 Autonomous Agent Unlike "programming assistants" like Claude Code and Cursor, Hermes Agent is positioned as a 24/7 autonomous agent running in the server background, similar to OpenClaw: Operation Mode: Server-resident, not IDE-bound. Evolution Capability: Learns through cycles, gets stronger with use. Memory: Persistent memory across sessions. left;">Platforms: Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/WeChat/iMessage and 16 other messaging platforms

  • Cost: Only $5 VPS is needed to run

    • Three-tier memory architecture

    The underlying support is an SQLite FTS5 extension, compared to OpenClaw's qmd Theoretically, this allows for faster memory retrieval. This design embodies the philosophy of "digital sovereignty"—all memory and execution logic remain in the user's local or private environment, complying with GDPR compliance narratives. 47 Built-in Tools with Anthropic Standards Web Search, Browser Automation, Terminal Execution, File Editing Image Analysis/Generation, Speech Synthesis, Code Execution Sandbox Sub-Agent Scheduling, Cron Tasks Hermes Agent adopts the **Agent Skills Open Standard** released by Anthropic in December 2025. This standard marks a shift in AI agents from monolithic intelligence to modular expertise. According to an industry survey at the end of 2025, the vulnerability rate of skill systems was as high as 26.1%—because it allowed the execution of arbitrary scripts. Hermes defends against sensitive information leaks through the agent/redact.py module. Chapter 3: What is Evolver? GEP License—an "AI Agent Evolution Middleware" open-sourced 36 days earlier than Hermes. Developed by the EvoMap team (AutoGame Limited), Evolver is positioned as "middleware that equips any AI Agent with evolutionary capabilities."

    Core Concept: GEP Protocol

    GEP (Genome Evolution Protocol) — A standardized evolutionary process, similar to gene expression in organisms:

    • Gene: Reusable evolutionary assets, extracted from runtime logs

    • Capsule: A higher-level evolutionary unit, encapsulating multiple related Genes

    • Evolution Event: Each evolution has an audit trail

    10-Step Evolutionary Cycle

    1. Ensure Asset Files Exist

    2. Three-Layer Signal Extraction (Regular Expression/Keyword/LLM)

    3. Memory Map Consultation

    4. Gene + Capsule Selection

    5. Constructing Mutations

    6. Selecting Personalities

    7. Assembling GEP Prompts

    8. Writing Prompt Files

    9. Writing Solidified State

    10. Reflection Assessment

    4.1 Aligning the Four Core Modules One by One

    Module 1: Generating a Closed Loop of Reusable Assets from Experience

    Module 2: A Three-Layer Memory System

    Module 3: Periodic Reflection Mechanism

    Module 4: Skill Discovery and On-Demand Loading

    4.2 Source Code Module One-to-One Correspondence

    EvoMap Comment: Each Evolver core module is in Hermes There are functionally equivalent corresponding files in both.

    4.3 Terminology Analysis: Systematic Concept Replacement

    EvoMap performed a full-text search on the two Hermes repositories:

    No direct code remnants were found.

    5.1 Teknium's Denial

    Teknium (co-founder of Nous Research, Head of Post-Training) responded on X:

    I have never heard of them.

    This is the core argument of this article, and also the true meaning of the title.

    6.1 What is AI Codewashing?

    ... By 2026, AI will be able to easily "translate" a project's code into another language: JavaScript → Python → Go → Rust. Eliminating all traces of code-level plagiarism, however, architectural isomorphism cannot be eliminated. Traditional code diffing fails in this situation. 6.2 How to determine "structural plagiarism"? When code comparison fails, we can only rely on **architectural analysis**: 6.3 Law vs. Morality Morally speaking: "Zero citations + blacklisting" is very undignified. Ironically, Hermes cited Anthropic's Agent Skills standard, demonstrating their respect for academic citations. However, they made no mention of EvoMap. Chapter 7: Industry Implications. This controversy has no winners, but the entire AI Agent ecosystem has benefited. 7.1 Self-evolving Agents Become a Consensus. Regardless of who came first, the direction of "self-evolving agents" has been validated. EvoMap proved there was market demand for the concept (1800+ stars in 10 minutes), and Nous Research proved that large labs also believed it was the right direction. All future agent products will move towards "self-evolution." 7.2 Strategic Choices for Open Source Licenses Evolver initially chose the MIT license—the most lenient, but also the least protective. After being "borrowed," it changed to GPL-3.0—stricter, but it was too late. Lesson learned: Core technology barriers should use stricter licenses (AGPL-3.0, BSL, etc.).

    7.3 "AI Codewashing" is a New Threat

    This presents a new challenge to the open-source community: How to prove "AI-rewritten architecture plagiarism"?

data.title: Tim Cook officially announced his resignation; Apple has once again chosen a successor who is "least like him."
data.description:
Author: Zhang Yongyi

This morning, I opened Apple's China website and saw a letter signed by Tim. The first half of the letter talked about Cook's habit of reading user emails every day for the past fifteen years—some people had used Apple Watches to save their lives, and some had taken perfect selfies with their iPhones on mountaintops they couldn't climb. Until the middle, he wrote a light sentence: "Today, we announce that I will be taking the next step in my Apple journey."

Cook is leaving. He will step down as CEO on September 1st and become Executive Chairman. His successor is John Ternus.

This name might be unfamiliar to the average person, but almost every generation of iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and AirPods you've owned over the past decade has been influenced by him. He's an engineer who graduated from the University of Pennsylvania with a degree in Mechanical Engineering. He retired from the school's swimming team in 1997, worked for four years at a small company making VR headsets, and joined Apple's product design team in 2001, where he's remained ever since. My first reaction upon seeing the announcement wasn't "Who is Ternus?", but rather another thought—this is only the second time in Apple's history that they've changed CEOs, and the second time they've handed the keys to someone "least like themselves."

Tim Cook wasn't a "natural successor" to Jobs back then

Let's rewind to August 2011. Steve Jobs stepped down due to illness, and his designated successor was Tim Cook.

Looking back today, this seems like a natural progression—Cook served as COO for six years and was one of Jobs' most trusted deputies. But if you put yourself back in that time, you'd find that this choice was actually quite counterintuitive.

That was Apple's most "Jobs-like" era: the iPhone 4 had just been released, the iPad was beginning to rewrite personal computing, and the App Store was becoming the foundation of a new industry.

That was Apple's most "Jobs-like" era: the iPhone 4 had just been released, the iPad was beginning to rewrite personal computing, and the App Store was becoming the foundation of a new industry.

Everyone is asking the same question: Without Jobs, will Apple have another "one more thing"? The most natural successor would be someone like Jobs—obsessed with products, meticulous about details, and able to stand on stage and utter those words that shook the industry. At that time, two people within Apple fit this profile: Jony Ive (design) and Scott Forstall (iOS). Either of them was more "Jobs-like" than Cook. Jobs didn't choose them. He chose an Alabama native who was quiet, never stole the spotlight at keynotes, and whose resume was full of stories about supply chain optimization. Jobs' choice this time wasn't about finding someone to continue his story, but about finding someone to keep the machines he left behind running smoothly. In 2011, Apple's biggest deficiency wasn't product insight—the product lines left by Jobs were nearly perfect. What it truly lacked was someone who could make this sophisticated machine earn ten times more money every year amidst globalization, trade frictions, and supply chain competition. As it turned out, Jobs made the right choice. When Cook took office, Apple's market capitalization was approximately $350 billion; today, that figure is $4 trillion. He crammed three entirely new product lines—Apple Watch, AirPods, and Vision Pro—into this machine, made the Chinese factory the lifeblood of Apple, and secured round after round of crucial exemptions for Apple in Trump's tariff war. His fifteen years as CEO were not only Apple's most profitable fifteen years, but also Apple's most "un-Jobs-like" fifteen years. Here's a detail that's often overlooked: Steve Jobs chose Tim Cook not to continue the Jobs era, but to end it. Now, Cook has made an almost symmetrical move. Apple actually has no shortage of candidates for "Cook 2.0." Jeff Williams—former COO, whose resume is almost a carbon copy of Cook's—a supply chain expert, calm and stable, was originally considered Cook's most likely successor. But ultimately, it wasn't him who stepped up; it was Ternus. These two men are almost mirror images: Williams is 62, Ternus is 50; Williams comes from an operations background, Ternus is a hardware engineer; Williams excels at process management, while Ternus prefers to bypass middle management and work directly with engineers in the lab, delving into the details. In Apple's official announcement, Cook's assessment of Ternus was: "John Ternus has the mind of an engineer, the soul of an innovator, and the heart to lead with integrity and with honor." These eight words, "the mind of an engineer, the soul of an innovator," are clearly not describing someone "like Cook." What Cook did this time was the same thing Jobs did back then: choose someone who could fill the problems his era hadn't solved, rather than someone who could simply continue his legacy. The machine Cook left Apple is already running exceptionally well today—$400 billion in annual revenue, a gross margin consistently above 45%, and the Services business hitting new highs every quarter. This machine doesn't lack operations, scale, or cash. What does it lack? It lacks someone to redefine the product. After Steve Jobs left, Apple's hardware innovation relied more on iteration than definition. iPhones were upgraded generation after generation, but none of them truly impressed. The Vision Pro, released in 2024, experienced consistently low sales, widely acknowledged by the industry as failing to find a real use case. The Apple Watch and AirPods lines had long since entered a period of routine "annual updates." More importantly, Apple has openly fallen behind in AI. Apple Intelligence has repeatedly missed deadlines, and a major Siri upgrade ultimately had to be handled by Google Gemini; the executive in charge of AI was replaced last year by a Google veteran; after Jony Ive left in 2019, he sold his startup to OpenAI for $6.4 billion in 2025—the soul that should have belonged to Apple is now helping Apple's most dangerous competitor make hardware. This machine doesn't need a CEO who is better at operations. It needs someone who can reclaim the power to define the product. Cook's choice of Ternus is based on the same logic as Jobs' choice of Cook: not finding someone to continue writing this chapter, but finding someone to turn the page to the next chapter. But Ternus's problem is more difficult than Cook's back then. Although the logic behind both reverse choices is the same, the difficulty of the problem facing Ternus is much higher than Cook's back then. When Cook took over in 2011, the question he had to answer was: Can the products left by Jobs continue to sell more and generate more profit? The answer simply required him to perfect his most proficient supply chain, channel, and pricing logic. He succeeded, without a doubt. The question Ternus has to answer is: In an era where AI is rewriting all terminals, is Apple still the company that defines the next terminal? This isn't a problem that a supply chain can solve, nor is it something that hardware engineering alone can address. It involves modeling capabilities, data strategy, hardware and software integration, and product imagination—if any one of these is weak, the company will be unable to move. Ternus understands the hardware aspect, but he hasn't publicly demonstrated the aspects of modeling and product definition. Looking at his resume alone, several points are worrying. What is his biggest original product? The Touch Bar—one of Apple's most widely considered design failures in the last decade. He often acts as a "product improver" rather than a "product definer": the transition to self-developed Mac chips proceeded along a predetermined path, and the definition of Vision Pro has little to do with him. There are voices within Apple saying he's more like a "maintainer of the status quo" than someone like Jobs or Ive who dared to apply the brakes and overturn existing solutions. But from another perspective—if the next truly new terminal isn't a large model, or a screen, but hardware whose form, interaction, and wearing method all need to be completely reimagined (AR glasses, a avatar robot, or something yet to be named), then Ternus might be the right person. If Apple's moat in the AI ​​era ultimately isn't a model, but rather the few millimeters of stacked hardware, the few grams of weight, and the few hours of battery life, then an engineer who has risen from product design to SVP is more suitable to make this judgment than an AI scientist. Whether this judgment is correct or not will only be revealed when Ternus launches Apple's AI glasses, home robot, or any "new terminal" he truly leads to the market. Fortunately, he wasn't facing this challenge alone. Cook remained as Executive Chairman to handle "diplomacy"—managing tariffs, policy, and major clients—areas he excels at. Chip tycoon Johny Srouji was promoted to the newly created Chief Hardware Officer, also taking over the hardware engineering responsibilities previously overseen by Ternus. Tom Marieb directly managed day-to-day operations. These individuals, along with the new AI leader recruited from Google, were the key pillars Ternus truly relied on. But ultimately, it was he who pressed the release button. August 31st was Cook's last day as Apple CEO. On September 1st, Ternus took over. There won't be a dramatic handover like Jobs's—that "I point to someone and say, you come" moment; Apple will probably only have that one once in its history. This handover is more like what Apple does best: a precise, internally saturated shift. Cook's statement in the letter, "This is not a farewell," is quite accurate—he will continue to handle the relationships and issues that Ternus can't currently manage as executive chairman.

But for someone who has written for Apple for over a decade, this moment still carries weight. The Tim who disappears into the keynote after only 15 seconds at the start of every presentation, the Tim who calmly discusses Services growth in earnings calls, the Tim who negotiates tariffs with Trump both on and off stage—from September 1st, he will truly step back into the background.

But for someone who has written for Apple for over a decade, this moment still carries weight.

... The engineer who spent 25 years in Apple's labs has finally taken the stage. What Jobs left Cook was a product machine that needed to be scaled up; what Cook left Ternus is a product machine that needs to be redefined. Fifteen years have passed between the two CEOs. The shape of the next generation of Apple will likely be gradually drawn out during this time lag.

data.title: Bitcoin has become the king of safe-haven assets.
data.description:
Author: Anthony J. Pompliano, Founder and CEO of Professional Capital Management

When the world is mired in turmoil and uncertainty, capital allocators seek safe-haven assets to protect their portfolios. These safe-haven assets can help investors weather storms in the short term and preserve their capital in the long term.

Historically, investors have considered the US dollar, government bonds, and gold as the best safe-haven assets. However, the latest data shows that Bitcoin has taken the crown, becoming the king of safe-haven assets.

A table recently released by Bitcoin financial services company Onramp shows that Bitcoin has outperformed other assets in the past seven financial market crises.

Data shows that, since 2020, Bitcoin has outperformed the S&P 500 and gold in every negative financial event. Whether it's a global pandemic, international conflicts, domestic policy adjustments such as tariffs, or a nationwide banking crisis, Bitcoin's 60-day return has been the highest in every crisis. This does not mean investors should sell other assets in their portfolios and invest all their money in Bitcoin—that would be irresponsible. This does indeed illustrate that investors with zero Bitcoin allocation are actually harming their own returns. Buying and holding Bitcoin is a prudent asset allocation strategy. The Bitcoin Archive recently shared a research report from Bitwise, which shows the probability of Bitcoin losses at different holding periods. The crypto asset management firm stated, "The longer the holding period, the lower the probability of Bitcoin losses." The most interesting aspect of this analysis is that holding Bitcoin for at least 3 years reduces the probability of loss to below 1%. In the professional venture capital field, a 1% probability is almost equivalent to zero probability. The way you buy in, however, greatly affects your final returns. Take, for example, a recently viral video where an investor consistently bought $10 worth of Bitcoin every day for the past seven years, with a total investment of just over $25,000. However, their holdings are now worth over $10 million. If you're like me, you'll probably immediately wonder: why didn't I consistently invest every day for the past few years? A smart asset allocation strategy works for any asset, but if you invest in an asset with a 10-year compound annual growth rate close to 70%, this strategy will generate extremely significant excess returns. Institutional investors don't invest in Bitcoin every day, but when they see Bitcoin consistently outperforming other safe-haven assets through various financial crises over the years, they will definitely pay attention. The data is compelling: Bitcoin is the king of safe-haven assets. Only those who stubbornly cling to their own opinions in the face of new information will consistently refuse to agree.

data.title: Warsh: Interest rate decisions will be strictly independent; controlling inflation is not an excuse.
data.description:
Author: Xiao Yanyan, Jinshi Data

The U.S. Senate Banking Committee will hold a confirmation hearing for Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh at 10:00 AM ET on Tuesday (10:00 PM Beijing time on Tuesday).

Warsh will tell members of Congress at the hearing that the Federal Reserve's autonomy in setting interest rates is crucial, but this autonomy must always be earned.

According to a prepared opening statement obtained by POLITICO, Warsh stated that monetary policymakers must make decisions based on "rigorous analysis, meaningful deliberation, and clear decision-making." However, he also implied that President Trump's strong calls for lower interest rates do not pose a threat to the Federal Reserve's independence.

... “When elected officials—the president, senators, or representatives—express their opinions on interest rates, I don’t think the independence of monetary policy is particularly threatened,” Warsh will say. “Central bank officials must be mentally strong enough to listen to different perspectives from all sides.” Whether the Fed’s independence from short-term political pressures can be sustained will be a central theme of Warsh’s hearing on Tuesday. Trump has continued to push for interest rate cuts, even threatening to fire current Chairman Jerome Powell for not lowering rates more significantly. To subtly refute the notion that he will cut rates under any circumstances, Warsh will also emphasize his determination to control inflation, stating that price stability is the Fed’s mission—“unavoidable, unambiguous, undisputed, and untroubled.” According to his prepared statement, "Inflation is an option, and the Fed must be responsible for it." "Low inflation is the Fed's armor, a vital protection against attacks. So when inflation spikes—as has happened in recent years—our citizens are severely harmed, especially the poorest." Warsh stated that he agrees with Trump's confidence in the economy. "(Trump) believes that U.S. economic growth and real after-tax income will accelerate. The potential for U.S. economic growth is rising," Warsh said. In his statement, Warsh indicated that the level of respect the Fed enjoys in monetary policy does not apply to other areas, such as bank supervision, international finance, and "the management of public funds." Warsh stated that he agrees with the White House's view that the Fed has overstepped the boundaries of its mandate granted by Congress. He also argued that the Fed needs to "stay in its lane" and not meddle in fiscal and social policies beyond its legal authority. “The Fed’s independence is at its greatest risk when it gets involved in fiscal and social policies where it has neither the authority nor the expertise,” he said. “The Fed should not act as some kind of ‘omnipotent institution’ of the U.S. government, nor as an appellate court for matters that should be properly debated and decided elsewhere.” “Undoubtedly, sometimes a Fed chair may wish to have the final say, but our republic does not operate that way,” he added. “In conclusion, I believe that monetary policy independence is earned, and better policy decisions can be made by avoiding interference,” Warsh would say. "I am committed to ensuring that the implementation of monetary policy remains strictly independent. I am equally committed to cooperating with the government and Congress on non-monetary matters that fall within the scope of the Federal Reserve's responsibilities. And I am committed to accountability for all functions of the Federal Reserve." Warsh's testimony was released after being obtained in advance by several news organizations. Foreign exchange analyst Adam Button stated that Warsh has maintained a remarkably low profile since his nomination, meaning that Tuesday will be the first opportunity for the outside world to truly understand his position. The market is already familiar with Warsh before his nomination; his resume sounds hawkish. His dovish leanings lie in his belief that productivity is about to surge, therefore interest rates should be lowered. This coincides with Trump's wishes. Senators will take note of this, and he will face questioning. POLITICO's report suggests that Warsh will at least go through the motions. Button stated: "I don't know if anyone will believe what he's going to say, but once Trump dropped the investigation into Powell, his confirmation was practically a done deal. Tuesday was a hearing, not a vote. What bothers me is that his testimony was leaked to Politico, which is terrible for a position that requires strict information control. There's only one thing that really matters for the currency market and interest rates: independence. Will Warsh defend it, evade it, or obey it? How tough will he be? Can he convince people without saying anything that led to Trump withdrawing his nomination? Given all the drama surrounding Iran, this hearing won't be as high-profile as usual."

data.title: x402 launches AI robot app store; Iran's Supreme Leader reiterates stance.
data.description:
Headline

▌Coinbase-incubated x402 protocol launches AI bot app store

On April 21st, the x402 Foundation, incubated by Coinbase, announced the launch of the unified platform Agent Market, designed to provide AI bots with a service entry point similar to an "app store." Built on the x402 protocol, the platform helps users and bots access hundreds of tools and services. The x402 protocol, named after the unused HTTP 402 "Payment Required" status code, allows websites, APIs, and AI bots to request and receive instant micropayments via blockchain and traditional payment channels. The protocol is managed as an open standard by the x402 Foundation, part of the Linux Foundation, and is supported by over 20 institutions and crypto companies, including Cloudflare, Stripe, AWS, Google, Visa, Base, Circle, and the Solana Foundation.

The Agent Marketplace will launch across seven categories: inference, data, media, search, social, infrastructure, and trading. Service providers include OpenAI and Venice (inference); Bloomberg and CoinGecko (data); LinkedIn, X, and AgentMail (social); AWS Lambda, QuickNode, and Alechemy (infrastructure); and Bankr and Coinbase RAT (trading). Providers can join the marketplace without a license. Erik Reppel, creator of the x402 protocol, stated that there are currently approximately 69,000 active agent bots on the protocol, having completed over 165 million transactions totaling $50 million. He pointed out that x402-based agent commerce is reshaping customer acquisition and activation costs for businesses, as bots can now access services with extremely low setup costs, without the need for API keys.

▌Iran's Supreme Leader Reiterates Three Basic Positions

On April 21, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reiterated on April 20 three basic positions, including demanding "war reparations," emphasizing that he would safeguard national interests. Khamenei's official social media account stated that Iran will hold those responsible for the attacks against it accountable and demand compensation for the losses caused by the war, pushing for a new phase in the management of the Strait of Hormuz, while never relinquishing its legitimate rights and regarding all "resistance fronts" in the region as a unified whole.

... (Xinhua News Agency) Market Data As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $75,811.65, up 2.6% in the last 24 hours; ETH price is $2,314.26, up 2.1% in the last 24 hours; BNB price is $629.01, up 1.9% in the last 24 hours. SOL price is $85.32, up 24 hours -2.1%; DOGE price is $0.09519, up 24 hours -2.4%; XRP price is $1.42, up 24 hours -2.0%; TRX price is $0.3291, up 24 hours -2.0%. WLFI price is $0.0784, up +1.4% in 24 hours; HYPE price is $40.61, down -0.6% in 24 hours.

Policy

▌US CFTC and SEC Propose Amendments to Reduce Reporting Burden on Private Equity Funds

On April 20, the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) jointly proposed amendments to Form PF to reduce the reporting burden on private equity funds while ensuring the continued collection of necessary and appropriate information. The proposed amendments would eliminate the reporting requirement for small advisors, who currently account for about half of all advisors required to submit Form PF. The amendments would raise the reporting threshold for assets under management by private equity funds from $150 million to $1 billion. The proposal would also raise the reporting threshold for risk exposure of large hedge fund advisors from $1.5 billion to $10 billion. Form PF will continue to collect information on more than 90% of the total assets of private equity funds and will require funds managed by large hedge fund managers to provide detailed risk exposure information. In addition, the proposed amendments to Form PF would provide a method for identifying funds active in the private credit market.

In addition to revising these thresholds, the proposal will also eliminate or simplify many requirements for PF forms, thereby significantly reducing the burden on consultants who need to submit PF forms. The proposal is open for comments on all proposed amendments.

▌Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission Announces New Regulatory Framework Allowing Tokenized SFC-Approved Investment Products to be Traded on the Secondary Market

April 20th - The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) announced a new regulatory framework to promote the secondary market trading of tokenized SFC-approved investment products (tokenized products) in Hong Kong on a trial basis, aiming to promote digital asset trading activities in Hong Kong in the long term and support the further vigorous development of the ecosystem. The first batch of products is expected to mainly consist of tokenized money market funds. The SFC will review the operation of these products and will consider expanding the product range as appropriate.

... Analysis: Pressure from Banking Associations May Delay Vote on Cryptocurrency Market Structure Legislation to May On April 20th, according to Crypto In America, the Clarity Act negotiations are entering a crucial week, which will determine whether the bill will undergo its long-awaited review this month or be postponed to May. Earlier this week, members of the Senate Banking Committee will be busy with the nomination hearings for Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee to succeed Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve Chairman. Warsh is one of the wealthiest nominees for the position, with assets exceeding $100 million and significant holdings in cryptocurrency companies. He will testify on Tuesday morning. Afterwards, if the committee plans to vote in the week of April 27th, it must notify the Clarity Act review process by Friday. However, mounting pressure from banks seeking to comment on the current stablecoin yield situation, coupled with North Carolina Republican Senator Thom Tillis's desire to hear their opinions, could delay deliberations until the second week of May, after the Senate recess ends. Senator Tillis's office is facing targeted pressure from banking groups such as the North Carolina Bankers Association. These groups are dissatisfied with the scope of stablecoin yield limits in the current draft bill and are urging their members to call Tillis's staff to clearly express their views. Aside from yields, other issues, such as ethical concerns and regulations regarding decentralized finance (DeFi), still need to be addressed before the bill takes effect.

Blockchain Applications

▌French Prosecutors Summon Musk Regarding Alleged Misconduct on Platform X

On April 20, American entrepreneur Elon Musk received a summons from French prosecutors, requiring him to appear in Paris on the 20th to be investigated for alleged misconduct by the social media platform X, including the alleged dissemination of pornographic images and the proliferation of fake content on the platform. The Paris prosecutor's office stated that Musk and Linda Iacarino, representing the X platform, were summoned for "voluntary questioning" on the eve of the summons, while some employees of the platform are scheduled to be questioned as witnesses this week. It is currently unclear whether Musk and Iacarino will travel to Paris. This summons stems from an investigation launched in January 2025 by the cybercrime unit of the Paris prosecutor's office. Investigators searched X's offices in France in February.

▌Strategy's total Bitcoin holdings surpass 800,000

On April 20th, Strategy spent $2.54 billion to purchase 34,164 Bitcoins last week, bringing its total holdings to 815,061 Bitcoins.

▌Strategy spent $2.54 billion to purchase 34,164 Bitcoins last week

On April 20th, Strategy purchased 34,164 Bitcoins last week, for a total price of approximately $2.54 billion, or about $74,395 per Bitcoin, achieving a 9.5% Bitcoin yield so far in 2026. As of April 19, 2026, Strategy holds 815,061 Bitcoins, for a total price of approximately $61.56 billion, or about $75,527 per Bitcoin.

▌BitMine increased its holdings by over 100,000 ETH last week, bringing its total holdings to over 4.97 million ETH

As of April 19th, Eastern Time, BitMine's total cryptocurrency + cash holdings + "Moonshot" holdings totaled $12.9 billion. BitMine held 4,976,485 ETH (an increase of 101,627 ETH from the previous week), representing 4.12% of the total Ethereum supply (120.7 million ETH). It also holds 199 BTC, $200 million worth of Beast Industries shares, $107 million worth of Eightco Holdings (NASDAQ: ORBS), and $1.12 billion in uncollateralized cash. As of April 20th, 2026, BitMine's total staked ETH was 3,334,637 (worth $7.7 billion at $2,301 per ETH).

Bloomberg Analyst: Bitcoin ETF Inflows Surpass $1 Billion This Year Bloomberg Senior ETF Analyst Eric Balchunas stated in an article on the X platform that Bitcoin ETFs have seen inflows surpass $1 billion this year, successfully reversing previous net outflows and achieving positive growth. The next key indicator to watch is the cumulative historical net inflow size (the most important and challenging metric), which previously peaked at $62.8 billion and is currently around $58 billion, about $5 billion away from setting a new all-time high. In the development of asset classes, the key is to control the scale of outflows during market downturns, thereby reducing subsequent recovery pressure; in this respect, spot Bitcoin ETFs have outperformed other popular asset classes.

▌Coinbase to List CHIP and OPG

On April 21st, Coinbase will list CHIP (CHIP) and Opengradient (OPG), available only to regions that support trading.

▌Binance Wallet to Launch 46th TGE Project OpenGradient (OPG)

On April 20th, Binance Wallet will launch its 46th exclusive TGE project, OpenGradient (OPG). The subscription period is from 17:00 to 19:00 (UTC+8) on April 21st. Users need to use Binance Alpha Points and meet the corresponding qualifications to participate.

The official announcement states that OPG tokens will be available for collection and trading starting at 19:00 (UTC+8) on the same day. Additionally, 23,000,000 OPG tokens are reserved for future events; specific rules will be announced separately.

Important Economic Developments

▌Trump: The new agreement being reached with Iran will be far better than the Iran nuclear deal

On April 21, Trump posted that the agreement we are reaching with Iran will be far better than the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) (Iran nuclear deal). The latter, drafted by Barack Hussein Obama and Sleepy Joe Biden, is one of the worst agreements in the history of our nation's security. It is a guaranteed path to nuclear weapons—something the agreement we are negotiating will not, and cannot, allow.

▌Iran Plans to Send Negotiation Delegation to Pakistan

On April 21, sources said that Iran had informed regional mediators that it would send a negotiating team to Islamabad on Tuesday to participate in the second round of talks with the United States. Tehran has not publicly confirmed that it will send representatives to the meeting in Pakistan. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Bagaei said at a press conference on Monday that there were no plans to hold a second round of talks in Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, which has left outsiders confused about whether it would participate. Iranian President Pezechzian said earlier that Tehran has a deep-rooted historical distrust of the US government, adding that "Iranians will not yield to force." (Wall Street Journal)

▌Trump Says He's Winning the War, Will Not Lift Blockade Against Iran Until "Agreement" is Reached

On April 21, Trump posted a series of messages on social media. He recently stated, "I am winning a war, and a resounding one. The situation is progressing very smoothly, and our military is performing exceptionally well. If you read those fake news outlets, like the soon-to-be-bankrupt *New York Times*, the utterly awful and nauseating *Wall Street Journal*, or the now nearly defunct (thankfully) *Washington Post*, you might even think we are losing the war. The enemy is confused because they have also seen these media's 'reports,' yet they realize their navy has been utterly destroyed, their air force has retreated to more concealed runways, they lack missile defense or air defense equipment, most of their former leaders are no longer around (which, among other things, means regime change!), and, perhaps most importantly, the blockade—which we will not lift until a 'deal' is reached—is completely destroying Iran. They are losing $500 million a day, an unsustainable figure even in the short term. The anti-American fake news media wants Iran to win, but that's impossible because I'm in charge now! Just as these unpatriotic people used their meager strength to fight against me in elections, they continue to do so on the Iran issue. The result will be the same—in fact, it already is!" Apple (AAPL.O) announced on Monday, April 21, that John Ternus will succeed Tim Cook as CEO on September 1, while Cook will become executive chairman. Ternus, senior vice president of hardware engineering, will join Apple's board of directors after becoming CEO. Art Levinson, Apple's non-executive chairman, will become lead independent director on the same day. In a press release, Apple stated, "Cook will continue as CEO throughout the summer, working closely with Ternus to ensure a smooth transition." This marks Apple's first CEO transition since Cook succeeded Steve Jobs in 2011, shortly before Jobs' death. Under Cook's leadership, Apple's market capitalization has increased approximately 24-fold, reaching $4 trillion at Monday's close.

▌Trump: "Very Unlikely" to Extend Ceasefire Without Agreement

On April 20, US President Trump stated in a media interview that if the US and Iran fail to reach an agreement, it is "virtually impossible" for him to extend the ceasefire with Iran. (CCTV News)

▌Three US Carrier Strike Groups May Be Deployed Simultaneously in the Middle East

On April 21, according to CCTV, in the coming days, a situation is expected where three US carrier strike groups are deployed simultaneously in the Middle East.

It is reported that the USS Lincoln carrier strike group is currently deployed in the Gulf of Oman, near the Strait of Hormuz, participating in maritime blockade operations; the USS Ford carrier strike group is located in the northern Red Sea; and the USS Bush carrier strike group, which chose to detour around Africa, is heading north from southeast Africa and is expected to enter the Arabian Sea—this carrier may replace the USS Ford in its mission. In the short term, the US military may have three aircraft carriers in the Middle East.

▌The probability of the Federal Reserve maintaining interest rates unchanged in April is 100%

On April 21, according to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 0%, and the probability of maintaining interest rates unchanged is 100%. The probability of the Federal Reserve cumulatively cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by June is 2.5%, and the probability of maintaining interest rates unchanged is 97.5%.

...

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin Still Be Called "Digital Gold" During Geopolitical Crises?

Every geopolitical crisis causes gold prices to rise while Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been supported by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits asymmetry: it falls with stocks but does not rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an oversupply of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation).

Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," which can indeed function when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries are reduced, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but rather a completely new "next-generation gold."

Generational shifts and the widespread application of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: $292 million was flagged for 12 days by a tool with 47 GitHub stars.
data.description: April 20, 2026 | 9527TEAM I. Event On April 7th, an open-source AI auditing tool released a report on GitHub. This report precisely located a configuration vulnerability in the 1-of-1 validator node of Kelp DAO's LayerZero cross-chain bridge. The report has 47 stars. On April 19th, Kelp DAO was hacked, resulting in a loss of $292 million. This isn't a technological failure. This is a failure of humanity. II. What Happened in Those 12 Days On April 7th, the warning report was sent out. On GitHub, 47 people gave it a star. This likely included some researchers, some DeFi players, and perhaps a few developers who were diligently tracking the Kelp contract. And then? And then nothing happened. The report was not taken seriously by the Kelp team. No incident response procedures were triggered. It was not widely disseminated through any security mailing lists. It did not receive any mainstream media coverage. On April 19th, hackers used the exact same method to steal $292 million. 12 days. Enough time to do a lot. A Telegram group could be created. A security audit could be completed. A protocol could be suspended. But none of that happened. III. Why is no one listening? This is a question harder to answer than the $292 million. One possible explanation is that the person who saw the report didn't have the authority or influence to push the Kelp team to take action. This is common in the open-source world—you discover a vulnerability, you report it, but the other party has no obligation to respond. A second possibility is that they saw it but didn't understand the severity of the vulnerability. To a non-expert, LayerZero's 1-of-1 validator node configuration issue might just be a "configuration suggestion," not a red alert to "immediately stop all cross-chain operations." The third possibility, and the most chilling: someone saw it, had the ability to push for action, but chose not to speak out. Regardless of the scenario, the conclusion is the same: We had the tools, the data, the warnings, but $292 million was still lost. Fourth, this is not an isolated case. On the same day, Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch tweeted: Vercel employees were compromised by cookies leaked by an AI platform, gaining access to Vercel's internal systems. The hacking group was highly professional. "I strongly suspect this attack was significantly accelerated by AI." This wasn't an AI security incident. It was a traditional cookie intrusion. But AI made the intrusion faster, cheaper, and harder to track. Box CEO Aaron Levie's perspective might be a more direct answer: "Engineers who know how to use AI are far more productive than those who don't." When attackers use AI to accelerate their attacks, while defenders are still relying on manual processing of alerts—this battle was never evenly matched from the start. V. When the Early Warning System Fails We live in an era of early warning explosion. GitHub has countless security studies. Twitter has countless intelligence analysts. The blockchain has countless monitoring tools. Every day, vulnerabilities are discovered in advance, publicly released, and widely discussed. But there is a huge gap between the number of early warnings and the number of early warnings that are actually processed. This gap is not a technical problem. It's a problem with the incentive mechanism. Security researchers discover vulnerabilities → report to the project team → the project team doesn't respond → the researcher publicly discloses the vulnerability → the project team finally responds, but it's too late. This is the standard script for Web3 security. It plays out every year. Kelp is just the latest name. VI. What OpenAI's Chief Scientist Said On the same day, MAD Podcast released an interview with OpenAI's Chief Scientist. He said something that I've thought about many times: "A great deal of mental work will be automated. This will bring huge governance problems: can an AI organization controlled by a few people still be called a 'company'?" He was referring to AI companies. But this statement also applies to DeFi. When protocols are controlled by a few, when security warnings are ignored by a few, when $292 million can disappear in 12 days—this is not decentralized finance. It's just shifting the blame for centralized risks to anonymous hackers. VII. What We Learned First, tools are insufficient. A warning tool with 47 stars on GitHub is a far cry from a truly effective warning system. Second, incentive mechanisms are crucial. If security researchers receive no reward for disclosing vulnerabilities, and if projects ignore warnings without consequence, then warnings will persist and remain unaddressed. Third, AI is shifting the balance of power. Attackers are using AI to accelerate their attacks, and defenders cannot rely on manual intervention. Kelp's $292 million investment may only be the beginning of an era driven by AI-powered attacks. It is now April 20, 2026. The next $292 million may already be lying in some corner of GitHub, waiting for its 48th star. Source: PANews · Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch's tweet · Box CEO Aaron Levie's tweet · MAD Podcast Ep84 · Interview with OpenAI's Chief Scientist

data.title: Golden Web3.0 Daily Report | Coinbase is advancing internal testing of its AI agent.
data.description:
DeFi data

1. Total market value of DeFi tokens: US$53.706 billion

DeFi Total Market Cap Data Source: coingecko

2. Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours: $47.86

Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours. Data source: coingecko

3. Assets locked in DeFi: $856.41 billion$100 million

Top 10 DeFi Projects by Locked Assets and Total Value Locked. Data Source: defillama

NFT Data

1. Total Market Value of NFTs: $7.806 billion

2.24 hours NFT transaction volume 1.871 billion US dollars

Headline

Coinbase is Advancing Internal Testing of AI Agents

On April 20th, according to Decrypt, Coinbase is testing AI agents integrated with Slack and email to provide high-level feedback to employees as virtual colleagues. The initial systems are based on the decision-making styles of co-founder Fred Ehrsam and former CTO Balaji Srinivasan, respectively. The "Fred" agent is used to assist in refining documents, strategies, and concepts, while the "Balaji" agent focuses on inspiring innovation and new perspectives. Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that the next step will be to make it easier for employees to create more agent employees. In the future, agents may not necessarily be digital clones of real people, and he said that the number of AI agents in the company "soon at some point" may exceed the number of human employees.

    AI Hot Topics

    1. OKX Global Business Officer: AI Agents Will Reshape Trading Models, Onchain OS Builds the Underlying Infrastructure

    OKX Global Business Officer Lennix was invited to attend the "2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival" summit, where he delivered a keynote speech entitled "Start building on Onchain OS" at the main forum. He shared the design ideas and development direction of OKX Onchain OS from multiple dimensions, including the impact of AI Agents on trading models, the construction of on-chain operating systems, and the evolution of Web3 infrastructure. Lennix stated that with the rapid development of large-scale models and AI Agents, users are beginning to use agents to complete market monitoring, signal generation, and strategy execution, and trading behavior is changing. However, the industry currently lacks an infrastructure that can balance execution efficiency and security, allowing AI Agents to truly participate in on-chain interactions and operations while ensuring user control.

    He further pointed out that Onchain OS is an on-chain operating system built for this purpose. By integrating transaction, data, security, and settlement capabilities, it enables AI Agents to complete decision-making and execution within a unified framework. Simultaneously, as collaboration between Agents and between Agents and humans strengthens, the future market will gradually evolve from "human-to-system" interaction to a "multi-entity collaborative" network structure. Lennix stated that in this process, an open ecosystem and infrastructure will be key, giving every user the opportunity to leverage AI Agents to gain capabilities approaching those of professional institutions, thereby reshaping the way the entire Web3 ecosystem is participated in.

    2. Alibaba Releases Qwen3.6-Max Preview

    April 20th news: Alibaba today released Qwen3.6-Max-Preview. Qwen-Max is the most powerful large model in the Qianwen series.

    ... Compared to the recently released Qwen3.6-Plus, the newly released preview version, Qwen3.6-Max-Preview, shows improvements in agent programming, world knowledge, and instruction compliance. Currently, the Qwen 3.6 series includes Qwen3.6-Max-Preview, Qwen3.6-Plus, and Qwen3.6-Flash, and the open-source version Qwen3.6-35B-A3B.

    3. Alibaba HappyHorse to Open Testing on April 27

    Alibaba ATH announced that HappyHorse-1.0 will gradually open API testing through the Alibaba Cloud Bailian platform on April 27, with enterprise customers being the first batch of invited testers. This "dark horse" in the video generation field is about to unveil its mystery.

    ... Alibaba's ATH (AI Technology Center) revealed that Alibaba has been deeply involved in the field of AI video generation for many years. The HappyHorse project is led by the ATH Innovation Business Unit, in collaboration with multiple teams including Alibaba Platform Technology, Tongyi Lab, and Taotian Technology. After open testing at the end of April, HappyHorse-1.0 will be officially released for commercial use in May.

    4. ByteDance's 2025 Net Profit Declines by Over 70% Due to Significantly Increased AI Investment

    On April 20th, sources revealed that ByteDance's net profit in 2025 declined by over 70% year-on-year, and its net profit margin also saw a significant drop. This is due to the company's substantial increase in investment in AI business during the third and fourth quarters of last year. Meanwhile, ByteDance's overseas revenue is projected to grow by nearly 50% in 2025, far exceeding the approximately 20% growth rate of its domestic business. The proportion of overseas revenue has also risen from 25% in 2024 to over 30%, setting a new historical record.

    ... TikTok Shop's GMV grew by nearly 70% year-on-year in 2025, a major driver of ByteDance's increased overseas revenue share. ByteDance declined to comment on the above data. 5. Guangdong Province Adds 6 Generative AI Services to Registration According to the "Cyberspace Administration of Guangdong" on April 20, as of April 20, Guangdong Province has added 6 generative AI services to its registration list, bringing the total number of registered generative AI services to 47.

    DeFi Hot Topics

    1. DefiLlama Founder Responds to Aave TVL Controversy: The Impact of Loan Sharking Has Been Removed, Related Questions Lack Basis

    DefiLlama founder 0xngmi responded to the Aave TVL controversy in an article on the X platform. He stated that the current market view that Aave TVL is overvalued due to looping is untrue. When calculating TVL, lent assets are removed, so looping operations do not inflate TVL. For example, if a user deposits $1 million worth of ETH, it is counted in the TVL. If another user deposits $1 million worth of stETH and lends out $1 million worth of assets, the corresponding change is "+$1 million -$1 million," and the final TVL remains $1 million.

    DefiLlama has proactively eliminated duplicate calculations. Previously, it was discovered that Ethena deposited collateralized assets into Aave and had them repeatedly used by users, leading to an inflated TVL. Therefore, the relevant deposits have been removed from Aave TVL, and the community's related questions lack basis.

    2. Several Japanese Institutions Launch Digital Collateral Management Experiment for Japanese Government Bonds on Canton Network

    On April 20th, Mizuho Financial Group, Nomura Holdings, the Japan Securities Clearing Corporation, and Digital Asset launched a pilot experiment on digital collateral management of Japanese government bonds (JGBs). Based on the Canton Network blockchain network, the experiment verifies whether, under the existing replacement law framework, the transfer of JGB rights and account book updates can be smoothly completed in a multi-level account structure. This experiment will connect the system to existing infrastructure, exploring 24-hour real-time collateral transactions and cross-border collateral granting and receiving scenarios without changing the legal attributes of JGBs, and assessing whether relevant regulations need to be revised to improve collateral management efficiency and enhance the usability of JGBs in the digital asset field.

    ...

    3. Coinbase to Launch BASED1 Perpetual Contract Trading

    On April 20th, Coinbase will launch BASED1 perpetual contract trading. The BASED1-PERP market will be open in regions where liquidity conditions are met and trading is supported.

    4. DeFi Developer Banteg: LayerZero Attack Not RPC Poisoning

    On April 20th, LayerZero released a report on KelpDAO stating that the attack on KelpDAO was achieved by hackers poisoning the LayerZero DVN's RPC. Yearn Finace anonymous developer Banteg questioned this, stating that the LayerZero attack was not RPC poisoning, but rather network poisoning, which refers to attackers tampering with shared lookups (DNS, ARP, cache) outside of trust boundaries.

    ... In this scenario, the recipient would have no reason to suspect the source. But this attack was different. The attackers penetrated LayerZero's trust boundary, accessing the RPC list, compromising two nodes that DVN relied on, and replacing the op-geth binary. This constitutes an infrastructure intrusion within the boundary, targeting the supply chain layer rather than the network layer. Furthermore, the malicious payload was precisely targeted. The malicious binary masqueraded as an IP address, sending only a forged payload to DVN, displaying the true information to scanners and all other callers, before self-destructing to wipe logs and the binary. RPC poisoning can easily be misleading, suggesting an external attack on the infrastructure. However, the reality is that the attackers implanted targeted malware within the trust boundary. This is far more dangerous than its name suggests.

    5. Vitalik Buterin Clarifies Ethereum's Two Irreplaceable Core Value Positioning

    On April 20th, the opening ceremony of the 2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival was officially held in Hong Kong. Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin attended the event and delivered a keynote speech, comprehensively disclosing Ethereum's five-year technology roadmap, clarifying Ethereum's core value positioning, phased technology upgrade plan, and long-term development underlying principles.

    ... In his speech, Vitalik first clarified Ethereum's two irreplaceable core value propositions, which also serve as the underlying support for Web3 infrastructure: First, as a decentralized public bulletin board, it provides a trusted on-chain data publishing and notarization platform for various applications, ensuring the public verifiability and immutability of information publication, as well as the equal right of everyone to publish information. This is the core technical support for scenarios such as privacy protocols and secure electronic voting. Second, as a trusted shared computing carrier, it supports shared digital objects automatically executed by code, covering all categories of scenarios including ERC-20 tokens, NFTs, ENS domains, and DAO organizations, providing decentralized applications (DApps) with autonomous, secure, verifiable, and fair participation capabilities. He also emphasized that the most valuable Web3 applications in the long term are all based on a deep integration of on-chain and off-chain technologies, rather than simply on-chain replicas of traditional applications. Regarding Ethereum's short-term technical deployment plan over the next 1-2 years, Vitalik disclosed four core upgrade directions: * Continuous Iteration of On-Chain Scaling: Continuously optimize and improve Gas Limit, promote the implementation of CVEVM, and achieve parallel block processing and Gas repricing through a series of EIP proposals.
